title: selago hermannioides
intro: Selago hermannioides, also known as the blue mountain-sage or Cape mountain-sage, is a flowering plant species endemic to the Western Cape region of South Africa. It is a shrubby perennial herb with blue to purple flowers, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its antiseptic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. Selago hermannioides can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
